_rules.construction: RFC 6962 Merkle Tree Head over the run-head set. Interior node = sha256(0x01 || left || right) over the RAW 32-byte child digests; subtrees split at k = the largest power of two strictly less than n; no node duplication
_rules.leaf: leaf hash = sha256(0x00 || canonical({"head": <lowercase hex head>, "run_id": <run id>})) where canonical is the RFC-0001 s5 form (canon.json _rules): NFC-normalized strings, sorted keys, minimal escaping, no whitespace
_rules.ordering: leaves ascend by NFC-normalized run_id in UTF-16 code-unit order (ADR-0020; RFC 8785 s3.2.3) — the leaf sequence equals the key order of the canonically serialized heads object. Input is a map: duplicate run_ids are unrepresentable, and two run_ids colliding after NFC are an error
_rules.domain_separation: 0x00 prefixes every leaf preimage, 0x01 every interior preimage (second-preimage defense; RFC 6962)
_rules.empty: the root of the empty head set is sha256 of the empty string
_rules.single: the root of a one-leaf set is that leaf's hash
_rules.encoding: roots render as lowercase hex, like every hash in the system
_rules.payload: anchor.appended v1 payload = {"container": {"base_seq": <u64>, "first_event_id": <hex>}, "heads": {<run_id>: <head hex>, ...}, "merkle_root": <hex>} as canonical bytes with no omitted keys; merkle_root MUST recompute from heads by these rules; the "" entry of heads is the latest owner-scope event (RFC-0002 D4), present whenever an owner-scope event precedes the anchor (ADR-0024)
_rules.root_cases: each case computes the Merkle root over its heads map; expected_root is the golden. ordering-utf16-astral discriminates UTF-16 code-unit order from code-point order (U+10000 sorts first); nfc-run-id carries a DECOMPOSED run_id (e + U+0301) whose leaf hashes the NFC form
_rules.payload_cases: each case is a complete v1 anchor payload: the harness asserts the root recomputes from heads and equals merkle_root, and that the canonical serialization of the payload is byte-exact (canonical) with the stated sha256 — pinning the payload schema itself (ADR-0017/ADR-0024)
_rules.verify_cases: behavioral cases for the anchor VERIFIER contract (ADR-0024; errors.md ANCHOR_MISMATCH): each case feeds its events, in order, to the chain-verify reducer and asserts the resulting alarm list EXACTLY (count, code, detail term, structured expected/got fields), that broken stays empty, and that every run's held head equals its last event's event_id — anchor alarms freeze nothing. Event fixtures were built with the vector-verified SealEvent rather than an independent script: the OUTCOMES are the pinned content, not the fixture hashes (owner reading, WP-04c.1); a mis-sealed fixture self-diagnoses as a CHAIN_BROKEN alarm and fails the expected list
_rules.provenance: goldens computed by an independent throwaway script implementing these rules (process.md s5), never by the implementation under test; empty/single/two-leaf roots additionally hand-derived via printf | sha256sum

root_cases.name: empty-set
root_cases.note: MTH({}) = sha256 of the empty string. The pure function is total; the store still refuses to WRITE an anchor over an empty container (ADR-0024)

root_cases.name: five-leaves-split-rule
root_cases.note: n=5 splits k=4|1 (largest power of two < n), exercising the unbalanced RFC 6962 shape; includes the "" owner-scope chain as an ordinary leaf (sorts first)

root_cases.name: ordering-utf16-astral
root_cases.note: U+10000 (surrogate pair D800 DC00) sorts BEFORE U+FF61 in UTF-16 code-unit order though it sorts after by code point / UTF-8 bytes — the ADR-0020 discriminating pair; a code-point-sorting implementation derives f1c03eadd0d370fbad54a18b30d898417af854d85c5796bb634366759a17bf3c instead

root_cases.name: nfc-run-id
root_cases.note: the run_id is DECOMPOSED (e + combining acute U+0301); the leaf hashes the NFC form (U+00E9). A non-normalizing implementation derives 4808571d488fc61a28aaeaf9640e0f4f5c0813e1340399037c604d97a4700022 instead

payload_cases.name: anchor-payload-recomputes
payload_cases.note: a complete v1 anchor payload: container attests the ADR-0022 A1 base (seq 101, the annotated trace's first event_id); heads include the "" owner-scope entry; merkle_root recomputes from heads; canonical/sha256 pin the payload schema bytes themselves

verify_cases.name: malformed-payload-gates-first
verify_cases.note: heads:null with a well-formed container and the empty-set root: the schema-required gate fires FIRST, so the alarm is anchor_payload — never anchor_root via the empty-set root (ADR-0024). Fixture events sealed with the vector-verified SealEvent; the pinned content is this expected outcome, not the fixture hashes

verify_cases.name: tampered-head-one-alarm
verify_cases.note: a validly sealed anchor whose merkle_root does not recompute from the verifier's own heads: exactly one anchor_root alarm with expected (derived) and got (claimed) roots; the post-anchor run_a event still threads — the anchor is impeached, not the chain (ADR-0024; errors.md ANCHOR_MISMATCH)

verify_cases.name: wrong-base-one-alarm
verify_cases.note: correct root, base_seq claims 2 where the observed base is seq 1: exactly one anchor_base alarm carrying the full expected/got attestation (both seq and first_event_id ride the alarm whenever either mismatches); broken stays empty

verify_cases.name: anchor-as-first-event
verify_cases.note: the ADR-0024 corollary of the empty-store refusal, now vector-pinned: base is observed AS the anchor itself (owner R1), and no payload can attest the anchor's own event_id (a sha256 fixpoint), so anchor_base always fires. The claimed root is the empty-set root so the root check passes against the verifier's empty heads and the alarm is exactly the base one
